title: tetragonia lasiantha
intro: Tetragonia lasiantha, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōhūhū, is a type of edible succulent plant native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Tetragonia lasiantha include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, and can be consumed fresh as a leafy green vegetable.
